title: The Impact of Video Games on Learning
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The debate over the (0) educational value of video games has grown significantly in recent years. Supporters argue that games can develop (17) problem-solving skills, improve (18) concentration, and encourage (19) creativity. Some studies suggest that strategic games can enhance spatial awareness and (20) decision-making abilities.||However, critics warn that (21) excessive screen time may have negative effects on children's (22) development, particularly when it replaces physical activity or (23) interpersonal interaction. Teachers and parents must work together to ensure that gaming remains a (24) beneficial and controlled part of a child's education rather than a harmful distraction.

example.text: The debate over the (0) EDUCATIONAL value of video games has grown significantly in recent years. (EDUCATE)

questions.explanation: The compound noun 'problem-solving' combines 'problem' and the gerund form of 'solve'.
The noun 'concentration' is formed from 'concentrate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
The noun 'creativity' is formed from 'create' by replacing '-e' with '-ivity'.
The compound noun 'decision-making' uses 'decision', formed from 'decide' by replacing '-de' with '-sion'.
The adjective 'excessive' is formed from 'excess' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
The noun 'development' is formed from 'develop' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
The adjective 'interpersonal' uses the prefix 'inter-' and suffix '-al' combined with 'person'.
The adjective 'beneficial' is formed from 'benefit' by adding the suffix '-ial'.
